summary: Semiconductors are foundational to AI and represent Baker's highest-conviction long-duration theme across both public and private portfolios
summary_extended: Baker describes semiconductors as his 'first love' as both a private and public investor, framing the sector as foundational to his firm's highest-conviction long-duration investment theme centered on AI infrastructure. He argues that deep semiconductor expertise — built over his career — provides Atreides with a differentiated edge in identifying AI investment opportunities across both public and private markets. This is explicitly a structural, multi-year view rather than a near-term tactical trade, reflecting Baker's belief that semiconductors will remain the critical bottleneck and value-creation layer in the AI buildout. No specific companies, price targets, or timeframes were cited; the thesis is a thematic orientation statement rather than a stock-specific call.
detail: Baker describes semiconductors as his 'first love' as both a private and public investor and explicitly links semiconductor expertise to AI investment edge. This is a structural view, not a near-term trade.
confidence_evidence: Baker uses 'first love' language and ties semiconductors directly to his firm's forward strategy and his own peak performance years thesis.
quote_fragment: semiconductors are my first love, both as a private investor and a public investor
